title: potentilla × collina var. johanniniana
intro: Potentilla × collina var. johanniniana, also known as Johann's cinquefoil, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is a hybrid of Potentilla collina and Potentilla fruticosa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Johann's cinquefoil include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with various health conditions, such as digestive issues and skin problems. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine, particularly in European folk remedies.
